subject: physics
educational_personality: exceptional classroom teacher; cream textbook; diagram teaches

lesson.big_idea: An electric circuit is a complete path for current. A break anywhere stops the flow.

sections.title: Have You Wondered?
sections.body: Why does a torch stay dark when one wire is loose? Electricity needs a complete loop.

sections.title: Using the Diagram
sections.body: Follow Cell → Switch → Bulb → Wire back to the cell. If any link is open, the bulb stays off.

sections.title: Concept: Circuit
sections.body: A circuit is a closed path that lets electric current travel from the power source through components and back again.

sections.title: Say It Simply
sections.body: Think of current like a bicycle on a track: it only keeps going if the track is unbroken.

sections.title: In Real Life
sections.body: When fairy lights fail because one bulb is out, that broken bulb opens the series path.

sections.title: Worked Example
sections.body: Draw a cell, switch, and bulb in a loop. Open the switch and predict: current stops, so the bulb goes dark.

sections.title: Try This
sections.body: Point to the switch on the diagram and explain what open and closed mean for the bulb.

sections.title: Lesson Summary
sections.body: Current needs a complete circuit. A cell supplies energy; wires carry it; a switch can open or close the path.

sections.title: Think About It
sections.body: Where do you use a switch at home? Explain how it changes the circuit.

sections.title: Apply Your Learning
sections.body: Build a safe paper model of a circuit and label each part with its job.

diagram_package.title: Electric Circuits
diagram_package.caption: Cell → Switch → Bulb → Wire
diagram_package.explanation: This diagram teaches how the ideas in Electric Circuits connect.
diagram_package.practice_question: Point to Cell and explain what it provides in the circuit.
